rationale: The frozen candidate is a production EmbeddedKnowledge Lesson Format v1/schema v3 pack for PREM-WEL-001 version 0.4.2, risk tier standard, mapped to topic-welcome-course-journey. The current pack differs from candidate e7d864d9640266ea43650e397aff5308f3a1795e only by the three final review artifacts. Each counted artifact validates, approves that exact candidate and lesson identity, has no unresolved major or blocking finding, discloses a unique run and complete agent provenance, and has an exact non-dismissed GitHub COMMENTED transport by github:VSBDev on PR #22. Recomputed quorum is three approvals: one academic, one learning-design, and one accessibility-rights review; one accountable principal; three isolated review run IDs; and three declared model families/providers (Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI), satisfying standard-lesson-v2. The prior record's material concerns were resolved in the frozen candidate: assessment prompts and rubric expectations align; completion requires all ten criteria; feedback and recovery cover the required capabilities and boundary; and the production reader now exposes operable, labelled, session-only criterion self-checks with item and 10/10 status without claiming automatic scoring or saved progress. The academic review confirms syllabus alignment and bounded claims, the learning-design review passes the cold first-read and assessment-alignment gates, and the accessibility-and-rights review confirms the semantic self-check route, attribution, CC BY 4.0 adaptation record, agent-access record, privacy and non-clinical boundaries, and absence of third-party assets. The shared accountable principal is expressly permitted during the founding stage; the declared runs and providers are attested public provenance, not independently verified identities. With the limitations and record-quality note preserved below, no credible unresolved blocker remains, so merge is warranted after the permitted governance-only publication transition and final checks.
dissent: No empirical novice-learner study, inter-rater study, or direct screen-reader, touch-device, or full non-web export session is available; the approvals are expert artifact reviews and implementation evidence, not evidence that learners achieved the outcome.

The learning-design review observes that exact grader-facing criterion wording is exposed in the self-check controls; plain-language summaries in the lesson mitigate this, so it is retained as a future learner-testing question rather than a current change request.

The learning-design artifact records signedAt as 2026-07-20T00:00:00Z, before the candidate commit time, while its exact GitHub transport was submitted after the candidate at 2026-07-20T11:28:22Z and targets the correct commit. The adopted schema and policy require a valid date-time and equivalent transport but define no temporal-order rule; this is preserved as a record-quality limitation and is not treated as disqualifying.
conditions: Before merge, the maintainer must make only the policy-authorized governance transition: set lesson.json.status to published, resolve lesson.json.sourceConfidence from pending-review, and set every retained claims.json claim reviewStatus to reviewed.
After that transition, rerun the strict source preflight and repository verification. Any teaching-content or substantive metadata change requires a new candidate and fresh review cohort.
